answer: Tokyo's daily budget ranges from $60 to $1,357 per person, depending on lifestyle and preferences. Budget travelers can manage on $60-$90, while luxury seekers may spend up to $1,357. Accommodation, food, and transport vary significantly.

results.title: How Much Does Tokyo Cost? 2026 Budget Guide | Prices & Tips
results.content:
Tokyo offers a surprisingly wide spectrum of price points, with daily budgets ranging from $60 / ¥9,567 for solo budget travelers to over $1,357 / ¥216,530 for those seeking high-end luxury. While it has a reputation for being an expensive metropolis, it is often more affordable than major Western hubs like New York or London, especially regarding high-quality, low-cost dining. Visitors can enjoy a filling meal of ramen for just $7.26 / ¥1,158 or navigate the city efficiently using affordable transit passes. The cost of a visit is heavily influenced by the choice of accommodation, which can jump from a $35 / ¥5,539 hostel bed to a $915 / ¥146,032 luxury suite. Strategic planning around local food chains and public transportation makes the city accessible even on a modest budget. [...] $1.9 / ¥303

## Payment & Money

How to pay and what to know about money

Japanese Yen (JPY)

1 $ ≈ 160 ¥

Yes

Widely accepted in malls, hotels, and chains, but small ramen shops and shrines remain cash-only.

Tipping is NOT practiced and can be considered awkward or even offensive. Good service is already covered by standard pricing.

7-Eleven (7-Bank) ATMs are the most reliable for foreign cards and are available 24/7. Fees are usually ~$0.7–$1.41 / ¥112–¥224.

### Connectivity

eSIM: $8.2–$26 (opens in new tab) [...] ### Is Tokyo expensive to visit?

Tokyo is a city of extremes where a traveler can get by on $60–$1,357 / ¥9,567–¥216,530 per day depending on whether they choose humble ramen stalls or world-class luxury hotels.

results.title: How Much Money to Bring to Tokyo? Complete Budget Guide 2026 | AirAsia MOVE
results.content:
Traveling in Tokyo can suit every budget. On a budget, expect to spend around ¥1,500–¥4,000 per day, covering basic transportation, inexpensive meals, and free or low-cost attractions. For a mid-range experience, daily expenses typically range from ¥6,000–¥12,000, allowing for comfortable transport, meals at casual restaurants, and entry to popular attractions. Those seeking a comfortable or premium trip can plan on ¥14,000–¥25,000+ per day, enjoying taxis or private transfers, higher-end dining, and multiple paid attractions. This breakdown helps travelers plan realistically based on their style and preferences. [...] Traveling in Tokyo can suit every budget. On a budget, expect to spend around ¥1,500–¥4,000 per day, covering basic transportation, inexpensive meals, and free or low-cost attractions. For a mid-range experience, daily expenses typically range from ¥6,000–¥12,000, allowing for comfortable transport, meals at casual restaurants, and entry to popular attractions. Those seeking a comfortable or premium trip can plan on ¥14,000–¥25,000+ per day, enjoying taxis or private transfers, higher-end dining, and multiple paid attractions. This breakdown helps travelers plan realistically based on their style and preferences.

## Budget-Friendly 5-Day Tokyo Itinerary with Estimated Costs [...] Here’s a realistic estimate for a 3–5 day trip in Tokyo

| Category | Budget (¥) | Mid-range (¥) | Comfortable (¥) |
 ---  --- |
| Transportation | ¥500 – 1,000 | ¥2,500 – 5,000 | ¥6,000 – 10,000+ |
| Food & Drinks | ¥1,000 – 2,000 | ¥2,500 – 5,000 | ¥6,000 – 10,000+ |
| Attractions | ¥0 – 1,000 | ¥1,000 – 2,000 | ¥2,000 – 5,000+ |
| Total / Day | ¥1,500 – 4,000 | ¥6,000 – 12,000 | ¥14,000 – 25,000+ |

results.title: Tokyo Budget Guide: How Much to Spend Per Day in 2026 | JapanTripCost.com
results.content:
This guide breaks down exactly how much Tokyo costs per day in 2026, with real prices for accommodation, food, transport, and activities. Use our Japan Trip Cost Calculator to get a personalized Tokyo budget.

## Tokyo Daily Cost Summary

| Category | Budget | Mid-Range | Luxury |
 ---  --- |
| Accommodation | $40 | $120 | $350 |
| Food | $20 | $50 | $120 |
| Transport | $8 | $15 | $40 |
| Activities | $10 | $30 | $80 |
| Daily Total | $78 | $215 | $590 |

## Accommodation in Tokyo

### Budget: $30-50/night

Tokyo has an excellent hostel scene. Top picks include Nui. Hostel in Kuramae, Khaosan World in Asakusa, and Book and Bed (sleep in a bookshelf!). Dorm beds run $20-30, private rooms $35-50. [...] A suggested 3-day Tokyo budget at mid-range:

 Accommodation: 3 nights × $120 = $360
 Food: 3 days × $50 = $150
 Transport: 3 days × $15 = $45
 Activities: 3 days × $30 = $90
 Total: $645 (excluding flights)

## Is Japan Expensive for Tourists?

Tokyo is more affordable than most visitors expect — comparable to cities like Barcelona or Berlin, and cheaper than London, Paris, or New York. Budget travelers can explore Tokyo on $60–$90/day, while mid-range travelers spend $150–$250/day. The weak yen makes 2026 an especially good time to visit.

## How Much Yen Do I Need Per Day in Tokyo? [...] | Activity | Cost | Worth It? |
 --- 
| Senso-ji Temple | Free | ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ |
| Meiji Shrine | Free | ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ |
| Shibuya Crossing | Free | ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ |
| teamLab Borderless | $25-30 | ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ |
| Shibuya Sky | $14 | ⭐⭐⭐⭐ |
| Tokyo Skytree | $15-25 | ⭐⭐⭐ |
| DisneySea | $75-90 | ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ |
| Robot Restaurant | Closed | N/A |
| Tsukiji Outer Market | Free (food extra) | ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ |
| Golden Gai (bars) | $8-15/drink | ⭐⭐⭐⭐ |

🎟️ Skip the Lines

Book teamLab, DisneySea, and Shibuya Sky tickets in advance on Klook. Many sell out weeks ahead.

## How Many Days in Tokyo?

3-4 days is the sweet spot for first-timers. You can see the highlights without rushing. If you love food, shopping, or nightlife, 5 days is even better.

A suggested 3-day Tokyo budget at mid-range:

results.title: Japan Budget Itinerary 2026: 10 Days Under $100/Day Complete Planning Guide
results.content:
$100 daily (approximately ¥15,000 at 2026 exchange rates) provides comfortable budget travel covering hostel accommodation ¥3,500-5,000, three meals ¥3,000-4,500, local transport ¥2,000-3,000, attraction admissions ¥1,000-2,000, and ¥1,000-2,500 buffer. This budget requires disciplined choices—hostel dorms over hotels, convenience stores over restaurants, free shrines over paid museums, and regional trains over taxis—but eliminates constant penny-pinching anxiety through realistic daily allowances. Budget travelers consistently report ¥12,000-18,000 daily spending when tracking expenses, making ¥15,000 achievable through strategic planning. The calculation breaks down: accommodation represents 23-33% of budget (¥3,500-5,000), food 20-30% (¥3,000-4,500), transport 13-20% (¥2,000-3,000), [...] Day one-two total costs: Narita Express/Skyliner ¥2,520-3,300, hostel x2 nights ¥7,000-10,000, meals ¥6,000-8,000 (day 1 dinner ¥1,500-2,000, day 2 breakfast ¥500-700, lunch ¥800-1,200, dinner ¥1,200-1,800, snacks ¥1,000-1,300), transport ¥800-1,200 (Yamanote Line rides, Suica card initial load ¥2,000 covers 3-4 days), possible Shinjuku Gyoen ¥500. Total days 1-2: ¥17,820-24,200 ($120-162), averaging ¥8,910-12,100 daily—slightly over $100 target due to arrival transport amortizing across trip.

## Day 3-4 Tokyo Western Areas and Day Trip Options 2026 [...] Attraction admissions total ¥1,000-2,000 daily (7-13% of budget) emphasizing free shrines/temples supplemented by selective paid entries. Major free attractions include Fushimi Inari shrine, Meiji Shrine, Senso-ji Temple (outer grounds), Tokyo Imperial Palace East Gardens, Osaka Castle park (exterior grounds), Yasaka Shrine, and neighborhood exploration. Paid admissions average ¥400-600 per temple (Kiyomizu-dera ¥400, Kinkaku-ji ¥500, Todai-ji ¥600) with strategic 2-3 paid entries daily (¥800-1,800) when interior access or gardens justify costs. Budget allocates ¥1,500 average attractions daily (¥15,000 total) covering 20-25 temple/museum entries across 10 days while maintaining majority time in free locations.

results.title: Japan Travel Costs 2026: Tokyo & Kyoto Budget
results.content:
This guide gives you real 2026 prices for accommodation, food, transport, and activities – plus essential tips for making your yen go further.

## Quick Overview: What Does Japan Really Cost in 2026?

Budget traveler: ¥10,000–15,000 ($65–100 USD) per day

Mid-range traveler: ¥20,000–35,000 ($130–230 USD) per day

Luxury traveler: ¥50,000–100,000+ ($330–650+ USD) per day

Average 10-day trip (mid-range): $3,500–5,500 USD total (including flights)

Important for 2026: Japan has introduced several new fees for international tourists, including a tripled exit tax (¥3,000) and dual pricing at some attractions. We've factored these into our calculations.
